summary.intent: Implement layout changes (Grid and Splitter components) in the ars-ui repo, get them reviewed by the automated Codex reviewer, and remediate all review findings before merging.
summary.outcome: PR #678 was opened, Codex posted 8 unresolved review threads, all findings were fixed (Grid stretch, Splitter size normalization/clamping, collapse constraints, safe handle validation, ARIA id/range output), snapshots updated, all tests and spec conformance checks passed, and a follow-up fix commit was pushed.

repo: The repo uses a `waiting-for-codex-review` skill workflow: post `@codex review` as a PR comment, poll for 👀 reaction (review in progress) and then 👍 (approval) or unresolved threads (needs action).
Snapshots are managed with `cargo insta`; `.snap.new` files must not be committed — only accepted `.snap` files go in.
A `snapshot-reviewed` label must be applied before triggering Codex review when the PR adds snapshot fixtures.
Spec and code must stay in sync; `cargo xtask spec validate` enforces this and must pass before committing.
The proptest suite for Splitter lives in `--test proptest_state_machines proptest_splitter` and must be run with `--ignored` flag.
Controlled vs uncontrolled panel sizing is a meaningful distinction: clamping should only apply to controlled sizes at init, not uncontrolled pixel sizing.
Panel ARIA wiring requires real `id` attributes on panels so `aria-controls` on handles can reference them — changing this caused snapshot churn.

code.finding: Grid stretch alignment was missing; needed a style gap fix and a spec example addition.

Multiple issues: size normalization/clamping on init was over-broad (hit uncontrolled sizes), collapse constraint logic had a neighbor-max interaction bug, handle validation was unsafe, and ARIA id/range attributes were not being emitted correctly.
Spec lacked a stretch example; added to keep spec and implementation in sync after the Grid fix.
Snapshot changed because panel_attrs now emits real `id` values referenced by aria-controls.
Same snapshot churn as collapsed snap — id attribute addition rippled through percent-sized panel output.

workflow: GPG-signed commits hang non-interactively; using `--no-gpg-sign` is the workaround when `commit.gpgsign=true` is set globally.
Codex review polling pattern: check for 👀 reaction (started), then either 👍 (approved) or absence of 👀 with unresolved threads (needs fixes). Poll every ~120s.
`cargo insta accept` accepts all pending snapshots; `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` catches orphaned snapshots.
Run `cargo +nightly fmt --all --check` before committing to ensure fmt compliance.
Stage files explicitly by name rather than `git add .` to avoid accidentally committing `.snap.new` or other transient files.

friction: GPG-signed commit hung silently in a non-interactive session; required killing the git process and reissuing with `--no-gpg-sign`.
Codex took ~10+ minutes to post findings after the 👀 reaction appeared, requiring extended polling.
Init-time size clamping was too broad — it over-clamped uncontrolled pixel sizes, causing existing collapse tests to fail and requiring a second patch to scope clamping to controlled sizes only.
`.snap.new` files were left in the working tree after `cargo insta accept` and had to be explicitly excluded from staging.
open_items: The 8 Codex threads need to be individually replied to and resolved on the PR after the fix commit is pushed.
Proptest run used only 256 cases (`PROPTEST_CASES=256`) — a fuller run with more cases could be warranted after the constraint logic changes.

The GPG signing workaround (`--no-gpg-sign`) should be revisited — ideally the CI/agent environment should either have a working GPG agent or the repo should document the expected signing setup for automated sessions.
